channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: SpaceX IPO explained: The price is set, but retail allocation still up in the air
item.description: Charles Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi and Morgan Stanley's E-Trade are among the brokerage platforms making SpaceX shares available.

item.title: U.S. military launches strikes in retaliation for Iran downing helicopter
item.description: The latest clash with Iran over the Strait of Hormuz came as President Donald Trump repeated that a deal with Tehran could be reached within days.

item.title: The May inflation numbers are due out Wednesday morning. Here's what to expect
item.description: If the Wall Street consensus is correct, the consumer price index is expected to show inflation running at a 4.2% annual rate.

item.title: Anthropic releases Mythos-like AI model to the public two months after private rollout rocked Wall Street
item.description: Anthropic said the broad release is possible because of new safeguards that block responses in specific high-risk areas.

item.title: These dividend stocks have slashed their payments, Morgan Stanley says
item.description: Shares of some companies could turn around if they clean up their act after cutting their dividend payments.

item.title: Kalshi rolls out whistleblower services, employment verification to curb insider trading
item.description: The platform will require traders to disclose their employer's identity when trading on sensitive markets, a Wall Street Journal article first reported.

item.title: Semiconductor shorts pile on as winning trade reverses
item.description: Investors' favorite trade is suddenly the one they can't stop betting against.

item.title: GM eyes new battery chemistry to grow AI data center, energy storage business
item.description: GM is expanding efforts to capitalize on the expected growth of energy storage and data centers and the development of next-generation sodium-ion batteries.

item.title: Kalshi trading in 'perps' crosses $1 billion in volume within a week of launch
item.description: A spokesperson for the company said that the product is the fastest growing in the prediction market platform's history.

item.title: Trump family got about $500M from crypto venture — but investors saw steep losses
item.description: Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. celebrated a deal with Alt5 Sigma that generated roughly $500 million for the Trump family. The company's shares are down 93%.

item.title: As LIV Golf faces a Saudi funding cliff, CEO says to take PIF 'at their word'
item.description: PIF is set to stop funding the golf league after the 2026 season, though LIV's Scott O'Neil could not guarantee that the final four tournaments will take place.

item.title: FIFA pres. on ticket prices: The World Cup being in America is a 'once-in-a-lifetime opportunity'
item.description: CNBC’s Sara Eisen sits down with FIFA President Gianni Infantino and AB InBev CEO Michel Doukeris to discuss FIFA’s World Cup sponsorship deal with AB InBev, the tournament’s economic impact, concerns over weak hotel bookings, and more.

item.title: White House border czar Tom Homan blames New York Gov. Hochul for promised ICE surge
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul in May signed a New York law that barred agreements with ICE that made it easier for the agency to detain immigrants who had been jailed.

item.title: Social Security retirement trust fund may be depleted in 2032, new trustees report finds
item.description: The Social Security Administration has released a report with new projections as to when the trust funds that help pay benefits may be depleted.

item.title: Investing in growth stocks isn't like betting on horses. Here's one new trade idea from Todd Gordon
item.description: In horse racing, once the thoroughbreds are in the gate, your bet is locked. There's nothing you can do to manage risk from that point forward.

item.title: $70 billion immigration funding package passes in U.S. House in a win for Speaker Johnson
item.description: The U.S. House on Tuesday approved a $70 billion immigration enforcement funding package after months of debate.

item.title: Apple shares slide after big Siri AI reveal
item.description: Apple unveiled new artificial intelligence software at WWDC, highlighted by its long-awaited update to Siri.

item.title: Rivian is betting on its R2 EV to turn the automaker into a household name like Tesla
item.description: The R2 SUV is meant to transform Rivian from a niche EV manufacturer that sells luxury vehicles into a more mainstream brand like U.S. EV leader Tesla.

item.title: JPMorgan Chase plans to deploy more powerful AI agents this year
item.description: JPMorgan Chase's move suggests long-running AI agents are close to clearing the security and governance hurdles that have slowed adoption inside big companies.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's former assistant Lesley Groff interviewed by House panel
item.description: Groff worked for Epstein for nearly 20 years, and her name appears more than 150,000 times in the Epstein files released by the Department of Justice.

item.title: Another jump in Boeing deliveries shows why we got into the stock and want to stay in
item.description: It's key to assessing the aircraft manufacturer's turnaround story under CEO Kelly Ortberg.

item.title: Capital One unveils 3 limited-time card offers: Earn up to $250 in bonus cash back
item.description: The spending requirements are low for the Capital One Savor, Savor Student and Quicksilver Student welcome bonuses, making them unusually easy to earn.

item.title: Home sales surged in May to the highest level since December
item.description: Home sales rebounded in May as mortgage rates dropped back a bit in April, but prices are still rising.

item.title: U.S. customs agency, trade judge to seek path to final tariff refunds
item.description: The U.S. Customs and Border Protection agency head to a federal trade court find a way to refund the tariffs that were deemed illegal by the Supreme Court.

item.title: Bitcoin's brutal sell-off sparks a flurry of trading in related stocks, including one big bullish bet
item.description: The year has been a rocky one for the flagship cryptocurrency, but traders aren't backing away from the space.

item.title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright says traffic in Strait of Hormuz is rising 'very meaningfully'
item.description: Wright said that describing the volume of oil exports out of Hormuz as increasing is a "fair statement."

item.title: USDA Secretary Rollins calls Texas ag chief 'unserious' amid screwworm threat
item.description: Texas Agriculture Commissioner Sid Miller lost his bid for a fourth term in a March Republican primary, despite Trump's endorsement.

item.title: Stellantis tells owners of 1.3 million Jeeps to park outside over fire concerns
item.description: Stellantis said the recall covers 2021 through 2025 model-year Jeep Wrangler SUVs and Jeep Gladiator trucks.

item.title: 5 takeaways from airline CEOs' biggest annual gathering
item.description: Airline CEOs met in Rio de Janeiro at IATA, the International Air Transport Association's annual meeting.

item.title: Boeing jet deliveries jump to 60 in May, 33% higher than last year
item.description: Boeing's May deliveries included 51 737 MAX jets, the highest number in one month since restarting production in December 2024 following a strike.
